label: Mohenjo-daro
sub: UNESCO World Heritage Site; major Indus Valley city (~2600–1900 BCE)

label: Harappa
sub: Indus Valley Civilization city; excavated from 1920s; gives name to the civilization

label: Taxila (Takshashila)
sub: Ancient city; one of world's earliest universities; Gandhara civilization center

label: Islamabad
sub: Capital of Pakistan since 1967; purpose-built city replacing Karachi as capital

label: Karachi
sub: Pakistan's largest city and financial capital; original capital 1947–1959; Jinnah's home city

label: Lahore
sub: Punjab capital; cultural heart; Lahore Resolution (1940) passed here; Badshahi Mosque

label: Rawalpindi (GHQ)
sub: Pakistan Army General Headquarters; seat of military power; where Bhutto and Liaquat were killed

label: Peshawar
sub: KPK capital; APS massacre 2014; gateway to Afghanistan; major TTP target

label: Quetta
sub: Balochistan capital; alleged 'Quetta Shura' Taliban HQ; BLA insurgency epicenter

label: Gwadar
sub: CPEC deep-water port; China-Pakistan Economic Corridor terminus; strategic Arabian Sea access

label: Khyber Pass
sub: Historic mountain pass between Pakistan and Afghanistan; used by Alexander, Mughals, British; now key trade route

label: Line of Control (Kashmir)
sub: De facto border dividing Kashmir between Pakistan-administered AJK and Indian-administered J&K since 1949 ceasefire

label: Muzaffarabad (AJK Capital)
sub: Capital of Azad Jammu & Kashmir (Pakistan-administered); disputed territory claimed by both states

label: Chagai (Nuclear Test Site)
sub: Site of Pakistan's May 28, 1998 nuclear tests (Ras Koh hills); 5 devices detonated at Chagai-I

label: Kahuta (KRL)
sub: Khan Research Laboratories; center of Pakistan's uranium enrichment and nuclear weapons program since 1970s

label: Abbottabad
sub: Site of Osama bin Laden compound; US Navy SEAL raid May 2, 2011; near PMA Kakul

label: Swat Valley
sub: Former TTP stronghold under Mullah Fazlullah (Radio Maulana); military operation Rah-e-Rast 2009

label: Waziristan (FATA)
sub: Former FATA tribal area; TTP heartland; US drone strikes 2004–2018; Operation Zarb-e-Azb 2014

label: Lal Masjid (Islamabad)
sub: Red Mosque; site of 2007 siege; key catalyst for TTP insurgency; central Islamabad

label: Siachen Glacier
sub: World's highest battlefield; disputed between India and Pakistan since 1984 Operation Meghdoot; ~8,000m elevation

label: Kargil
sub: Site of 1999 Kargil Conflict; Pakistani infiltration of Indian positions; nuclear-armed nations' clash

label: Durand Line (Af-Pak Border)
sub: 1893 British colonial border; not recognized by Afghanistan; splits Pashtun territories; major security flashpoint

label: Badshahi Mosque, Lahore
sub: Mughal-era mosque (1673, Aurangzeb); symbol of Muslim heritage; capacity 100,000; near Lahore Fort

label: Thar Coal Fields
sub: World's 7th largest coal reserve; 175 billion tonnes; major CPEC energy component in Sindh

label: Mangla Dam
sub: Completed 1967; Pakistan's 2nd largest dam; key hydropower and irrigation source; World Bank funded

label: Tarbela Dam
sub: World's largest earth-fill dam; completed 1974; 4,888 MW capacity; critical for electricity and agriculture

label: Lahore Fort (Shahi Qila)
sub: UNESCO World Heritage Site; Mughal fortress built by Akbar; expanded by Jahangir, Shah Jahan, Aurangzeb

label: Faisalabad (Lyallpur)
sub: Pakistan's 3rd largest city; textile industry capital; formerly Lyallpur under British rule

label: Hyderabad (Sindh)
sub: Former Talpur Mir capital of Sindh; British conquest (1843); major commercial center

label: Multan
sub: City of saints (Sufi shrines); one of the world's oldest cities; major Punjab cultural center
